changes.before: Guards posted outside the dungeon mouth crossed themselves before taking the stairs down.
changes.after: Guards crossed themselves before taking the stairs.

changes.before: the stale bakery smell that always clung to Jaina after hard spellwork, now fouled by ash and old wool and the copper edge of blood swallowed too often
changes.after: bread, ash, and the copper edge of blood swallowed too often

changes.before: No. It depended on whether anyone in the chamber above wanted truth before they heard what shape it took.
changes.after: cut to let the clerk's questionnaire carry the bias

changes.before: Written before he came in. The order gave it away.
changes.after: Written before he came in. Not a man seeking sequence.

changes.before: By the time she reached the sword singing after Arthas hit the throne steps, the clerk had the flat mouth of a man enduring madness.
changes.after: The pen moved hard through the first part. Slowed at Stratholme. Slowed again at Northrend.

changes.before: Real.
changes.after: cut

changes.before: A raised bench loomed over the flagstones with three dark chairs, stacked papers, and enough polished wood to make every word sound official.
changes.after: A raised bench with enough polished wood to make every word sound official.

changes.before: a man who had not slept enough and had still taken the time to tie every knot on his robes correctly because disorder anywhere else in the room was going to have to work around him
changes.after: a man who had not slept enough and had still tied every knot on his robes correctly

changes.before: Warnings issued beyond proper authority. Unauthorized travel. Contacts with foreign magi. Hidden correspondence. Armed intrusion into the palace interior.
changes.after: Unauthorized travel. Contacts with foreign magi. Armed intrusion into the palace interior.

changes.before: The speed with which Sylvanas and Jaina had reached correct conclusions along the grain road became proof they had known the shape of events in advance.
changes.after: Relay reports became preparation.

changes.before: Ashcombe kept the room moving, but Thornton was the one checking each cited page with a finger and leaving it on the record. He never looked hurried. He wanted order, and Ashcombe was feeding it to him one neat line at a time.
changes.after: He checked each cited page with a finger and left it on the record. Ashcombe was feeding him order one neat line at a time.

changes.before: The torch sconces along the wall guttered without any draft the hall should have had.
changes.after: The torch sconces guttered.

changes.before: Worse than white. Locked.
changes.after: Locked.

changes.before: A guard at the gallery door shifted his weight away from the center table and then froze when he realized others had seen him do it.
changes.after: cut to keep the blade focus on the bench, clerk, and silver paste

changes.before: He spoke as paladin and veteran both: plain, grave, and with no ornament the dead had not earned.
changes.after: cut; Uther's testimony already carries the register

changes.before: The damaged blade. The final arrow. Custody taken after.
changes.after: The final arrow.

changes.before: Each true answer gave Thornton one cleaner line on the page and left Uther less room to fight from the chair.
changes.after: Each true answer gave Thornton one cleaner line on the page and left Uther less room to fight.

changes.before: For one heartbeat Sylvanas thought the blow had landed cleanly enough to matter.
changes.after: For one heartbeat Sylvanas thought it might matter.

changes.before: he had found the end of whatever leash Dalaran had placed around his throat and discovered it was iron
changes.after: he had found the end of Dalaran's leash and discovered it was iron

changes.before: Knew Vereesa's breathing. Knew the tiny hitch before one sentence. Knew the cost of keeping the paper from shaking.
changes.after: Knew Vereesa's breathing. Knew the tiny hitch before one sentence.

changes.before: tiny pressures along the iron, too small for anyone who had not shared walls and battlefields with her to notice
changes.after: Jaina had begun counting against her thumb with one chained hand.

changes.before: Thornton kept reading in the same level voice while the room absorbed the first blow.
changes.after: Thornton kept reading in the same level voice.

changes.before: Jaina's face had gone blank with a body's refusal to spend itself on the smaller catastrophe while the larger one was still upstairs in a box.
changes.after: Jaina's face had gone blank while the larger catastrophe was still upstairs in a box.

changes.before: Whatever span men with seals imagined while they passed orders up staircases and argued jurisdiction over the dead
changes.after: Whatever span men with seals imagined while they passed orders and argued jurisdiction over the dead

changes.before: Two Farstriders waited back in the dark behind him, and farther off the human guards had yielded him only so much corridor.
changes.after: Two Farstriders waited back in the dark behind him.

changes.before: callused, cold, familiar as old field gear
changes.after: callused, cold, familiar

flags: Dialogue hunt checked the clerk, Ashcombe, the palace mage, Uther, Antonidas, Vereesa, Lor'themar, and the Sylvanas/Jaina wall exchange; no spoken-line offenders like 'actionable,' 'vector,' 'optimal,' or 'primary route' appeared.
Line 191: 'as if they were carrying a bier' kept — concrete courtroom image, and the narration stays under the chapter cap for comparison tics.
Line 201: the mage's 'as if the cold got inside them' was kept as witness-language under pressure, not memo register.
Line 99: 'Something inside the oak-and-lead chest' was kept because the POV genuinely cannot identify the moving part from below the vault.
Line 439: Lor'themar's 'Humans improve once condemned women become someone else's problem' was kept — bitter, plain, and in-register for a trusted elf under pressure.
Line 509: 'Hold.' was kept — command shorthand consistent with Sylvanas/Lor'themar field language, not clipped-for-clipped's-sake AI dialogue.
